response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software developer with web, mobile, and some embedded background, but the role requires deep hardware/silicon expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, no DNN training/inference optimization, no SoC modeling, and no relevant PhD/MS in a hardware-adjacent field. The skill overlap with a Principal Performance Architect role is minimal.
strengths: Senior-level tenure at large tech companies (Google, Meta) with broad engineering experience
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang microservices and C/C++ at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testability, and developer tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware experience
No demonstrated deep-learning workload analysis or DNN training/inference optimization background
Educational background (BS CS + Fine Arts) does not meet PhD+3 / MS+6 / BS+8 hardware-engineering expectation for a Principal Architect role

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN optimization background
Senior IC tenure at top-tier tech companies
